history: The client has a history of recurrent stomachaches and physical symptoms linked to anxiety and stress. They have experienced past difficulties with weight management, sexuality, and academic performance, which may have contributed to self-criticism and fear of inadequacy. The client reports a significant transformation during college, suggesting a period of personal growth and increased self-efficacy. There is a pattern of seeking control in relationships, possibly as a way to manage anxiety about uncertainty or instability.
intermediate_belief: If I lose control or things change, I will end up unhappy again. If I am not vigilant, I could slip back into my old, unhappy self. I must always maintain positive aspects of my life or I will fail.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't keep things together, it means I'm a failure and things will never get better.
coping_strategies: The client seeks control in relationships and may engage in reassurance-seeking or over-planning to manage anxiety. They may avoid situations that remind them of past difficulties or could threaten their current stability, which limits opportunities for growth. Rumination and worry about losing positive aspects of life are also present.
situation: Concern about losing positive aspects of life or returning to a previous unhappy state.
auto_thought: If I don't keep everything under control, I'll lose what makes me happy and end up miserable again. I can't let myself slip back to how things were before.
behavior: I overthink and try to plan for every possible problem. I avoid situations that feel risky or remind me of my past struggles. I seek reassurance from others to make sure things are still okay.
